title: Lemonade (LMND) Full Year 2024 Earnings: EPS Beats Expectations
text: Lemonade delivered mixed results for fiscal year 2024. The company generated $526.5 million in revenue, representing 23% growth compared to the prior year. However, the insurer continued operating at a loss, though improving its financial trajectory. The net loss contracted to $202.2 million, down 15% from 2023. Per-share losses improved notably to $2.85, compared to $3.40 in the previous year—a meaningful 16% improvement. The earnings announcement impressed Wall Street in one key metric. While revenue aligned with expectations, earnings per share surpassed analyst estimates by 6.4%. This beat suggests the company is making progress toward profitability despite remaining unprofitable. Management projects stronger expansion ahead. Over the next three years, revenue is expected to grow at an annual rate of 21%, substantially outpacing the broader insurance industry's projected 5.2% growth. Investor sentiment turned negative following the announcement. The stock declined 14% in the week after earnings results were released. The company identified 3 warning signs that warrant investor consideration, though specific details were not disclosed in this report.
